title: SUB GEOGRAPHY TOPIC 1 SOIL 2 AGRICULTURE PARMAR 4

questions.question: 1. What is the study of soil called? / मिट्टी के अध्ययन को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Pedology is the scientific study of soils, including their formation and classification.

questions.question: 2. Which soil horizon is known as the 'Topsoil'? / किस मृदा संस्तर (horizon) को 'टॉपसॉयल' के रूप में जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: A Horizon is the topsoil, typically dark in color and rich in organic matter.

questions.question: 3. The process of minerals washing away or percolating underground is called? / खनिजों के बह जाने या जमीन के नीचे रिसने की प्रक्रिया क्या कहलाती है?
questions.explanation: Leaching occurs when water washes important minerals down through the soil profile.

questions.question: 4. Which soil is the most widely spread and important soil of India? / भारत की सबसे व्यापक और महत्वपूर्ण मिट्टी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Alluvial soil covers about 40% of India's total area, mostly in the northern plains.

questions.question: 5. 'Khadar' refers to which type of alluvial soil? / 'खादर' किस प्रकार की जलोढ़ मिट्टी को संदर्भित करता है?
questions.explanation: Khadar is new, fertile alluvial soil deposited by floods annually.

questions.question: 6. Which soil is also known as 'Regur Soil'? / किस मिट्टी को 'रेगुर मिट्टी' के नाम से भी जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Black soil is also called Regur or Black Cotton soil because it is ideal for cotton.

questions.question: 7. Black soil is rich in which of the following nutrients? / काली मिट्टी निम्नलिखित में से किस पोषक तत्व से भरपूर होती है?
questions.explanation: Black soil is rich in lime, iron, magnesium, and calcium carbonate but poor in nitrogen.

questions.question: 8. Which soil develops a reddish color due to the diffusion of iron? / लोहे के प्रसार के कारण किस मिट्टी का रंग लाल हो जाता है?
questions.explanation: Red soil looks red due to iron in crystalline and metamorphic rocks; it looks yellow when hydrated.

questions.question: 9. Laterite soil is formed due to which process? / लैटेराइट मिट्टी का निर्माण किस प्रक्रिया के कारण होता है?
questions.explanation: Laterite soil forms in tropical areas with high rainfall through intense leaching.

questions.question: 10. Which soil is best for growing Cashew nuts? / काजू उगाने के लिए कौन सी मिट्टी सबसे अच्छी है?
options: Red Laterite Soil / लाल लैटेराइट मिट्टी
questions.explanation: Red laterite soils in Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Kerala are suitable for cashew crops.

questions.question: 11. 'Kankar' layers are found in which soil due to increasing calcium content? / कैल्शियम की मात्रा बढ़ने के कारण किस मिट्टी में 'कंकड़' की परतें पाई जाती हैं?
questions.explanation: In Arid soils, the lower horizons are occupied by Kankar because of increasing calcium.

questions.question: 12. Saline soils are also known as? / लवणीय मिट्टी को किस नाम से भी जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Saline soils are infertile and contain more salt; they are known as Usara soils.

questions.question: 13. Peaty soils are generally found in areas of? / पीटी (पीली) मिट्टी आमतौर पर किन क्षेत्रों में पाई जाती है?
options: High rainfall and high humidity / भारी वर्षा और उच्च आर्द्रता
questions.explanation: Peaty soils occur in areas of heavy rainfall and high humidity where vegetation growth is good.

questions.question: 14. What is the process of covering topsoil with plant material called? / ऊपरी मिट्टी को पौधों की सामग्री से ढकने की प्रक्रिया क्या कहलाती है?
questions.explanation: Mulching involves covering soil with organic matter like leaves to retain moisture.

questions.question: 15. Which erosion occurs when topsoil is washed away by heavy showers? / भारी बौछार के कारण ऊपरी मिट्टी के बह जाने पर कौन सा अपरदन होता है?
questions.explanation: Sheet erosion removes the entire top layer of soil over a large area.

questions.question: 16. 'Gully erosion' leads to the formation of? / 'गली अपरदन' से किसका निर्माण होता है?
questions.explanation: Gully erosion cuts deep channels, making the land unfit for cultivation, known as badlands.

questions.question: 17. Which state is famous for Terrace farming? / सीढ़ीदार खेती के लिए कौन सा राज्य प्रसिद्ध है?
questions.explanation: Terrace farming is practiced in hilly regions like Uttarakhand to prevent soil erosion.

questions.question: 18. 'Shelter belts' are most effective in preventing? / 'रक्षक मेखला' किसे रोकने में सबसे प्रभावी हैं?
questions.explanation: Shelter belts are rows of trees planted to break the force of the wind.

questions.question: 19. Which element is generally deficient in almost all Indian soils? / लगभग सभी भारतीय मिट्टियों में आमतौर पर किस तत्व की कमी होती है?
questions.explanation: Most Indian soils, including alluvial and black soil, are generally poor in nitrogen.

questions.question: 20. Soils found in the Himalayan region are typically? / हिमालयी क्षेत्र में पाई जाने वाली मिट्टी आमतौर पर कैसी होती है?
questions.explanation: Forest soils are found in hilly and mountainous areas where sufficient rain forests are available.

questions.question: 21. In which state are the Chambal ravines located? / चंबल के बीहड़ किस राज्य में स्थित हैं?
questions.explanation: The Chambal basin in MP is famous for ravines created by gully erosion.

questions.question: 22. What is the ideal pH for most crop plants? / अधिकांश फसल पौधों के लिए आदर्श pH क्या है?
questions.explanation: Most crops grow best in slightly acidic to neutral soil (pH 6.0 to 7.5).

questions.question: 23. Which soil is best for 'Basmati Rice' in North India? / उत्तर भारत में 'बासमती चावल' के लिए कौन सी मिट्टी सबसे अच्छी है?
questions.explanation: Fertile alluvial soil with good irrigation is perfect for Basmati rice production.

questions.question: 24. Soils that are mostly made of organic matter like peat are? / पीटी जैसे कार्बनिक पदार्थों से बनी मिट्टी कौन सी होती है?
questions.explanation: Histosols are soils primarily composed of organic materials, often found in wetlands.

questions.question: 25. Strip farming involves alternating crops to? / स्ट्रिप फार्मिंग में फसलों को बारी-बारी से उगाना किसके लिए होता है?
options: Reduce water / पानी कम करने के लिए
Increase height / ऊंचाई बढ़ाने के लिए
Attract pests / कीटों को आकर्षित करने के लिए
Prevent erosion / अपरदन रोकने के लिए
questions.explanation: Strip farming breaks the force of wind and water by alternating different types of crops.

questions.question: 26. Self-ploughing is a characteristic of which soil? / 'स्व-जुताई' किस मिट्टी की विशेषता है?
questions.explanation: Black soil develops deep cracks when dry, which helps in aeration, called self-ploughing.

questions.question: 27. Which soil is found in areas with high temperature and heavy rainfall? / उच्च तापमान और भारी वर्षा वाले क्षेत्रों में कौन सी मिट्टी पाई जाती है?
questions.explanation: High temperature and heavy rain lead to the formation of laterite soil through leaching.

questions.question: 28. Nitrogen fixation in soil is primarily done by? / मिट्टी में नाइट्रोजन स्थिरीकरण मुख्य रूप से किसके द्वारा किया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Bacteria like Rhizobium in legume roots fix atmospheric nitrogen into the soil.

questions.question: 29. Which factor determines soil color and mineral content? / कौन सा कारक मिट्टी के रंग और खनिज सामग्री को निर्धारित करता है?
questions.explanation: Parent rock is the primary material that determines the chemical properties and color of soil.

questions.question: 30. 'Bangar' soil is less fertile than 'Khadar' because? / 'बांगर' मिट्टी 'खादर' की तुलना में कम उपजाऊ है क्योंकि?
options: It has more moisture / इसमें अधिक नमी होती है
It is new / यह नई है
It is found near rivers / यह नदियों के पास पाई जाती है
It contains more Kankar nodes / इसमें अधिक कंकड़ ग्रंथियां होती हैं
questions.explanation: Bangar is old alluvial soil, often containing calcareous deposits (Kankar), making it less fertile.

questions.question: 1. Which farming is also known as "Slash and Burn" agriculture? / किस खेती को "स्लैश एंड बर्न" कृषि के रूप में भी जाना जाता है?
options: Intensive Subsistence Farming / गहन निर्वाह खेती
Primitive Subsistence Farming / आदिम निर्वाह कृषि
questions.explanation: It is practiced on small patches of land using primitive tools like hoe and dao.

questions.question: 2. Shifting cultivation is known as 'Milpa' in which region? / स्थानांतरित कृषि को किस क्षेत्र में 'मिल्पा' के नाम से जाना जाता है?
options: Mexico and Central America / मैक्सिको और मध्य अमेरिका
questions.explanation: Shifting cultivation is called Milpa in Mexico and Central America.

questions.question: 3. What is the name of shifting cultivation in North East India? / उत्तर-पूर्वी भारत में स्थानांतरित कृषि का नाम क्या है?
questions.explanation: In North East India (Assam, Meghalaya, Mizoram), it is called Jhumming.

questions.question: 4. Which type of farming involves growing crops and raising livestock together? / किस प्रकार की खेती में फसल उगाना और पशुपालन एक साथ शामिल है?
questions.explanation: Mixed farming involves both crop production and animal husbandry on the same land.

questions.question: 5. What is 'Truck Farming' associated with? / 'ट्रक फार्मिंग' किससे संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Truck farming refers to the specialized production of vegetables for the market.

questions.question: 6. Which of the following is a Kharif crop? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी खरीफ की फसल है?
questions.explanation: Kharif crops like paddy and maize are sown with the onset of monsoon.

questions.question: 7. Rabi crops are typically sown in which season? / रबी की फसलें आमतौर पर किस मौसम में बोई जाती हैं?
questions.explanation: Rabi crops are sown in winter from October to December.

questions.question: 8. India is the ______ largest producer of rice in the world. / भारत दुनिया में चावल का ______ सबसे बड़ा उत्पादक है।
questions.explanation: India is the second largest producer of rice in the world after China.

questions.question: 9. Which gas is emitted from flooded rice fields? / जलमग्न चावल के खेतों से कौन सी गैस उत्सर्जित होती है?
questions.explanation: Anaerobic conditions in flooded rice fields lead to the emission of methane (CH4).

questions.question: 10. What are the three seasons of rice in West Bengal? / पश्चिम बंगाल में चावल के तीन मौसम कौन से हैं?
options: Jhum, Milpa, Roca / झूम, मिल्पा, रोका
Aus, Aman, Boro / आउस, अमन, बोरो
Penda, Podu, Bringa / पेंडा, पोडू, ब्रिंगा
Ravi, Kharif, Zaid / रवि, खरीफ, जैद
questions.explanation: In West Bengal, farmers grow three crops of paddy in a year: Aus, Aman, and Boro.

questions.question: 11. What is the ideal temperature for sowing wheat? / गेहूं बोने के लिए आदर्श तापमान क्या है?
questions.explanation: Wheat requires 10-15°C during sowing and 21-26°C during harvesting.

questions.question: 12. FRP (Fair and Remunerative Price) is associated with which crop? / FRP (उचित और लाभकारी मूल्य) किस फसल से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: The government announces FRP for sugarcane to be paid by sugar mills to farmers.

questions.question: 13. Which soil is best suited for Tea cultivation? / चाय की खेती के लिए कौन सी मिट्टी सबसे उपयुक्त है?
questions.explanation: Tea grows well in fertile, well-drained laterite soil rich in humus.

questions.question: 14. Which country is the largest producer of Tea? / चाय का सबसे बड़ा उत्पादक देश कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: China remains the leading producer of tea globally.

questions.question: 15. Rhizobium bacteria in leguminous crops help in? / फलियों वाली फसलों में राइजोबियम बैक्टीरिया किसमें मदद करते हैं?
questions.explanation: Leguminous plants like pulses help in restoring soil fertility by fixing nitrogen.

questions.question: 16. Which of the following is known as "Sri Anna"? / निम्नलिखित में से किसे "श्री अन्न" के रूप में जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Millets like Jowar, Bajra, and Ragi are now referred to as "Sri Anna".

questions.question: 17. Cotton is also known as which type of fiber? / कपास को किस प्रकार के रेशे के रूप में भी जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Cotton is called the "Silver Fibre" of India.

questions.question: 18. Which state is the topmost producer of Jute in India? / भारत में जूट का शीर्ष उत्पादक राज्य कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: West Bengal leads the production of Jute, also known as "Golden Fibre".

questions.question: 19. 'Sericulture' is the rearing of? / 'सेरीकल्चर' किसका पालन है?
questions.explanation: Sericulture is the commercial rearing of silkworms for the production of silk.

questions.question: 20. Who is known as the "Father of Green Revolution" in the world? / विश्व में "हरित क्रांति के जनक" के रूप में किसे जाना जाता है?
options: William S. Gaud / विलियम एस. गौड
questions.explanation: Dr. Norman Borlaug developed high-yield wheat varieties in Mexico.

questions.question: 21. The term "Green Revolution" was coined by? / "हरित क्रांति" शब्द किसके द्वारा गढ़ा गया था?
options: William S. Gaud / विलियम एस. गौड
questions.explanation: William S. Gaud was the first to use the term "Green Revolution" in 1968.

questions.question: 22. "Operation Flood" is related to which revolution? / "ऑपरेशन फ्लड" किस क्रांति से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Operation Flood (1970) aimed to increase milk production in India.

questions.question: 23. Who is the "Father of White Revolution" in India? / भारत में "श्वेत क्रांति के जनक" कौन हैं?
questions.explanation: Dr. Verghese Kurien is the mastermind behind the Amul model and White Revolution.

questions.question: 24. 'Pisciculture' refers to? / 'पिसिकल्चर' का तात्पर्य क्या है?
questions.explanation: Pisciculture is the breeding and rearing of fish in ponds or tanks.

questions.question: 25. Yellow Revolution is associated with the production of? / पीली क्रांति किसके उत्पादन से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Yellow Revolution focused on increasing the production of edible oilseeds.

questions.question: 26. Which revolution is associated with Fertilizer? / उर्वरक से कौन सी क्रांति संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Grey Revolution aims to boost fertilizer production and usage.

questions.question: 27. 'Viticulture' is the cultivation of? / 'विटिकल्चर' किसकी खेती है?
questions.explanation: Viticulture is the science and practice of growing grapes.

questions.question: 28. Blue Revolution is related to which sector? / नीली क्रांति किस क्षेत्र से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Blue Revolution refers to the rapid growth of fish and aquatic production.

questions.question: 29. 'Apiculture' is the practice of? / 'एपिकल्चर' किसका अभ्यास है?
questions.explanation: Apiculture is the maintenance of bee colonies, commonly in hives, by humans.

questions.question: 30. Agriculture Census in India is conducted every? / भारत में कृषि जनगणना प्रत्येक ______ में आयोजित की जाती है?
questions.explanation: The Agriculture Census is conducted every 5 years by the Ministry of Agriculture.
